title: calonema caesareum subsp. maritimum
intro: Calonema caesareum subsp. maritimum, also known as sea Calonema, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Orchidaceae. It is a type of orchid that grows in coastal areas and is characterized by its white or purple flowers. Calonema caesareum subsp. maritimum has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of this plant include treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues, and it can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for consumption.
